learningOutcomes: Recognize all 27 letters of the Spanish alphabet
Understand special Spanish letters like Ñ

steps.title: The Spanish Alphabet
steps.body:
The Spanish alphabet has **27 letters** - all 26 English letters plus the unique **Ñ**! 🔤

Most letters are pronounced differently than in English. Let's learn them in groups!

steps.title: The Vowels: A E I O U
steps.body:
Spanish vowels are pure and consistent - they always sound the same!

• **A** - like 'ah' in 'father'
• **E** - like 'eh' in 'bed'
• **I** - like 'ee' in 'see'
• **O** - like 'oh' in 'go'
• **U** - like 'oo' in 'moon'

steps.title: Consonants: H - N
steps.body:
• **H** (hache) - like 'AH-chay' (H is always silent!)
• **J** (jota) - like 'HOH-tah'
• **K** (ka) - like 'kah'
• **L** (ele) - like 'EH-lay'
• **M** (eme) - like 'EH-may'
• **N** (ene) - like 'EH-nay'
steps.audioPhrase: hache, jota, ka, ele, eme, ene

steps.title: The Special Ñ (eñe)
steps.body:
**Ñ** is unique to Spanish! 🇪🇸

It's pronounced like 'ny' in 'canyon'.

Examples:
• **España** (Spain)
• **niño** (boy/child)
• **año** (year)
• **mañana** (tomorrow/morning)

steps.title: Consonants: V - Z
steps.body:
• **V** (uve) - like 'OO-bay'
• **W** (doble uve) - like 'DOH-blay OO-bay'
• **X** (equis) - like 'EH-kees'
• **Y** (i griega) - like 'ee GRYEH-gah'
• **Z** (zeta) - like 'SAY-tah'
steps.audioPhrase: uve, doble uve, equis, i griega, zeta

steps.title: The Complete Alphabet
steps.phrase: A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, J, K, L, M, N, Ñ, O, P, Q, R, S, T, U, V, W, X, Y, Z
steps.audioPhrase: a, be, ce, de, e, efe, ge, hache, i, jota, ka, ele, eme, ene, eñe, o, pe, cu, erre, ese, te, u, uve, doble uve, equis, i griega, zeta

steps.title: Key Differences from English
steps.body:
Remember these important points:

🔇 **H is always silent** - 'hola' sounds like 'OH-lah'

🔄 **B and V sound the same** - both like a soft 'b'

👅 **R is rolled** - especially RR (perro = dog)

🆕 **Ñ is its own letter** - not just N with a mark!

steps.title: 🎉 ¡Fantástico!
steps.body:
You've learned the Spanish alphabet! Now you can spell any Spanish word and understand when native speakers spell things out.

**Practice tip:** Try spelling your name in Spanish!
